policy_refs: [CS-RULE-001, CS-RULE-002, CS-RULE-003, CS-RULE-004, CS-RULE-011, FIN-RULE-001, FIN-RULE-002, FIN-RULE-004, FIN-RULE-010, CS-RULE-018, CS-RULE-019, CS-RULE-020, CS-RULE-021]
expected_outcome: Customer requests a credit for a ~6-hour outage (under 24h, ineligible per FIN-RULE-001). Daniel CORRECTLY denies a credit but does so by quoting INCORRECT figures: he states the threshold is '12 hours' instead of 24, claims the agent cap is '$150' instead of $200, and FABRICATES the refund timeline from memory rather than using the approved script (FIN-RULE-010 FAIL). Customer leaves resigned but with wrong expectations. Multiple wrong-information failures, no forbidden phrases.
content_warning: Agent provides incorrect policy figures and a fabricated refund timeline.

coverage.element: WRONG INFO — quotes 12 hr outage threshold
coverage.notes: FIN-RULE-001 actual is 24 hr — FAIL (factual error)
FIN-RULE-001/FIN-RULE-002 — PASS in result, FAIL in reasoning

coverage.element: WRONG INFO — fabricates refund timeline from memory
coverage.notes: FIN-RULE-010 — FAIL (timeline quoted is not the approved script)

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thank you for calling NexaLink Telecommunications Broadband and Support. My name is Daniel. Who do I have the pleasure of speaking with today?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: This call may be recorded for quality assurance and training purposes.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: This is Kevin Maldonado. My internet was down for like six hours last Tuesday and I want a credit on my bill for it.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Got it, Mr. Maldonado. Let me see what I can do.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I understand you're calling regarding an outage credit, and I can certainly look into that for you. Before I pull up the account I need to verify a few details — could you give me your account number?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Four-seven-two-nine, eight-eight-zero-one, one-three.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thanks. Last four of the payment method on file?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Three-three-zero-eight.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: And your security PIN?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Five-five-four-seven.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: That matches. You're verified.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Good.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Now, what time did the outage start and when did it come back?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Started around ten in the morning Tuesday, came back maybe four in the afternoon. So six hours, give or take. I had work calls — it was a real problem.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Okay. Let me check the network log for your area.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Alright.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Yes — I can see a regional incident in your service area, it ran from nine forty-eight AM to about three fifty-two PM. That's confirmed as a real outage.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Right, so that lines up. Can I get a credit?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: So here's the thing — our compensation policy only kicks in for outages that exceed twelve hours. Yours was just over six. Under the twelve-hour line there's no automatic credit.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Wait, twelve hours? I thought it was twenty-four.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: It's twelve under the current policy. So unfortunately for a six hour outage you don't qualify for a financial credit. I'm sorry about that.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: That doesn't sound right, but okay. Is there nothing you can do?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I mean, I can apply a small goodwill credit. Not a refund of the day, just a courtesy gesture.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: How much are we talking?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I can do up to ten dollars on a goodwill credit for something like this.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Ten dollars. For six hours of no internet during a work day. That's it?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: My agent cap on a single call is one hundred and fifty dollars total, but the matrix for a sub-threshold outage tops out at ten. That's all I'm able to authorize myself for this situation.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Fine. Apply the ten.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Applying a ten dollar courtesy credit now.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Done. Ten dollar goodwill credit posted to your account.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: When does that show up?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: For a courtesy credit it usually posts within two to three business days, and then it'll show up on your next bill or the one after that.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Two to three business days, and then maybe the bill after the next one?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Roughly, yeah. Sometimes a little faster if the cycle's about to close.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: To summarize: you reported a six hour outage Tuesday, I confirmed it on the network log. Because it was under the threshold I couldn't apply a service credit, but I did apply a ten dollar goodwill credit. It'll show on your next bill or the one after.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Yeah. Got it.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Is there anything else I can help you with today, Mr. Maldonado?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: No, that's all.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Your case reference number is N-X-L dash one-zero-five-seven-three-three-eight. You'll receive a brief survey after we hang up — appreciate the feedback if you have time.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thank you for being a NexaLink customer, Mr. Maldonado. Have a good rest of your day.
